parts.contentJson: {"text":"I want to create a site that's optimized for web and mobile use. It is a move club site where you can create and join groups for movie clubs. In it you have your dashboard which displays clubs you're a part of along with recent movies you've watched/rated. Once you click into a club, it shows you the movie that you're watching for the current turn (you can cycle back and forth to other consecutive turns) which shows the movie and some information about it, along with a place for you to rate/review if you haven't already. You can also find the watch status of other members at a glance to know who has yet to watch (although you cannot see the rating they gave until the turn ends). Once a turn ends, the turn view transforms into a results view, where you just see the movie and the ratings/reviews of other members along with some overall club stats. If an admin re-opens rating/review window after a movie's turn has ended, the rate/review component appears above the results. Every turn there is a new person who picks the movie for that turn. You can access this picker schedule in the menu visible across all pages in a club. Only admins can edit this. There is also a movie suggestion option in the menu, which allows any member to add movies to a suggestion pool with credit attributed to them for nominating it. Finally, admins can see an Admin Panel in the menu which gives them the option to adjust turn schedules, pickers for any turn, movies for a specific turn, lock/unlock movie selection or review window. There is also a role management view for adjusting roles/permissions for users (or transferring club ownership). And some other group settings. The movie selection view is available to admins and the picker for that turn. Here they should see a search bar with auto-complete, along with the nomination pool for that group as submitted by other members. ","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"I'll create a comprehensive movie club site with dashboard, club management, turn-based movie watching, and admin controls. This requires backend persistence for user data, clubs, ratings, and permissions.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"This is a substantial web application for a movie club. Let me break down what the user is asking for:

1. Dashboard - shows clubs you're in and recent movies watched/rated
2. Club view - shows current turn's movie with:
   - Movie info
   - Rate/review component (if you haven't rated)
   - Watch status of other members (can see who watched, but not their ratings until turn ends)
   - Ability to cycle through turns
3. Turn end state - transforms into results view showing:
   - Movie
   - All ratings/reviews from members
   - Overall club stats
   - If admin re-opens rating window, rate/review appears above results
4. Menu (visible across all club pages):
   - Picker schedule (who picks which turn, admin-editable)
   - Movie suggestions pool (any member can add, shows who nominated)
   - Admin Panel (admin-only):
     - Adjust turn schedules
     - Change pickers for turns
     - Change movies for specific turns
     - Lock/unlock movie selection or review window
     - Role management (adjust permissions, transfer ownership)
     - Other group settings
5. Movie selection view (for admin and current picker):
   - Search bar with autocomplete
   - Nomination pool from members

This is classified as SupabaseRequired because it needs:
- User authentication
- Persistent data for clubs, members, movies, ratings, reviews
- Real-time updates for watch status
- Role-based permissions

I should invoke the supabase skill first to get guidance on how to handle this.","providerOptions":{}}
